question_text: Two electromagnets are made with identical iron cores and the same wire. Electromagnet A has 50 coils and carries 2A of current. Electromagnet B has 100 coils and carries 1A of current. Which electromagnet is stronger and why?
correct_answer: Electromagnet B is stronger because it has more coils (100 vs 50). Although it has lower current (1A vs 2A), the doubling of coils has a greater effect on magnetic strength than halving the current.
explanation: Electromagnet strength depends on both current and number of coils. Electromagnet B's 100 coils at 1A creates a stronger field than A's 50 coils at 2A because doubling the number of turns doubles the magnetic field strength, while the current is only halved.
